title: वास्तु दोष निवारण मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वास्तु दोष निवारण मन्त्र ॥

ॐ वास्तोष्पते प्रतिजानीह्यस्मान् त्स्वावेशो अनमीवो भवा नः।
यत्त्वेमहे प्रतितन्नो जुषस्व शं नो भव द्विपदे शं चतुष्पदे॥ १॥

वास्तोष्पते प्रतरणो न एधि गयस्फानो गोभिरश्वेभिरिन्दो।
अजरासस्ते सख्ये स्याम पितेव पुत्रान् प्रतिनो जुषस्व॥ २॥

वास्तोष्पते शग्मया संसदा ते सक्षीमहि रण्वया गातुमत्या।
आवः क्षेम उत योगे वरं नो यूयं पात स्वस्तिभिः सदा नः॥ ३॥

ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय।
वास्तुपुरुषाय विद्महे महाभूताय धीमहि।
तन्नो वास्तुः प्रचोदयात्॥ ४॥

वास्तुदेवं नमस्कृत्य गृहदोषनिवारणम्।
सर्वविघ्नोपशान्त्यर्थं मन्त्रमेतत् पठेत् सुधीः॥ ५॥

ईशानकोणे भगवान् शिवः पातु सदा गृहम्।
आग्नेयकोणे वह्निश्च नैर्ऋत्ये रक्षसां पतिः॥ ६॥

वायव्यकोणे पवनः पूर्वे इन्द्रः सुरेश्वरः।
दक्षिणे यमधर्मश्च पश्चिमे वरुणो विभुः॥ ७॥

उत्तरे कुबेरो धनदः ब्रह्मा मध्ये व्यवस्थितः।
अनन्तः पृथिवीशेषो अधः पातु सदा गृहम्॥ ८॥

सर्वे गृहदोषाः प्रशमं यान्तु विष्णुकृपाबलात्।
वास्तुपूजाप्रभावेन गृहे शान्तिर्भवेत् सदा॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री वास्तु दोष निवारण मन्त्र सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantra ||

Om Vastoshpate prati-janihy-asman tsva-avesho anamivo bhava nah |
Yat-tvemahe prati-tanno jushasva sham no bhava dvipade sham chatushpade || 1 ||

Vastoshpate pratarano na edhi gayasphano gobhir-ashvebhir-indo |
Ajarasas-te sakhye syama piteva putran prati-no jushasva || 2 ||

Vastoshpate shagmaya samsada te sakshimahi ranvaya gatumtya |
Avah kshema uta yoge varam no yuyam pata svastibhih sada nah || 3 ||

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya |
Vastu-purushaya vidmahe Maha-bhutaya dhimahi |
Tanno Vastuh prachodayat || 4 ||

Vastu-devam namaskritya griha-dosha-nivaranam |
Sarva-vighna-upashanti-artham mantram-etat pathet sudhih || 5 ||

Ishana-kone Bhagavan Shivah patu sada griham |
Agneya-kone Vahnish-cha Nairritye Rakshasam Patih || 6 ||

Vayavya-kone Pavanah Purve Indrah Sureshvarah |
Dakshine Yama-dharmash-cha Pashchime Varuno Vibhuh || 7 ||

Uttare Kubero Dhanadah Brahma madhye vyavasthitah |
Anantah Prithivi-shesho adhah patu sada griham || 8 ||

Sarve griha-doshah prashamam yantu Vishnu-kripa-balat |
Vastu-puja-prabhavena grihe shantir-bhavet sada || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantra Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे वास्तोष्पति! हमें स्वीकार करो, आप निवास में रोगरहित हों। जो हम चाहें वह स्वीकार करो, दो पैर और चार पैर वालों का कल्याण हो॥ १॥

हे वास्तोष्पति! हमारा तारणहार बनो, गो और अश्वों से समृद्धि दो। आपकी अजर मित्रता में रहें, पिता जैसे पुत्रों को स्वीकार करो॥ २॥

हे वास्तोष्पति! आपकी सुखमय सभा में, आनन्दमयी गति से हम रहें। क्षेम और योग दोनों में हमें वर दो, स्वस्ति से सदा रक्षा करो॥ ३॥

ॐ भगवान वासुदेव को नमस्कार। वास्तुपुरुष को जानते हैं, महाभूत का ध्यान करते हैं, वह वास्तु हमें प्रेरित करे॥ ४॥

वास्तुदेव को नमस्कार कर गृहदोष निवारण के लिए, सर्व विघ्न शान्ति हेतु बुद्धिमान यह मन्त्र पढ़े॥ ५॥

ईशान कोण में भगवान शिव, अग्नि कोण में अग्नि, नैर्ऋत्य में राक्षसों के पति सदा गृह की रक्षा करें॥ ६॥

वायव्य कोण में पवन, पूर्व में इन्द्र, दक्षिण में यमधर्म, पश्चिम में वरुण विभु॥ ७॥

उत्तर में धनद कुबेर, मध्य में ब्रह्मा, पृथ्वी के नीचे शेषनाग सदा गृह की रक्षा करें॥ ८॥

विष्णुकृपा के बल से सब गृहदोष शान्त हों। वास्तुपूजा के प्रभाव से गृह में सदा शान्ति हो॥ ९॥
benefits: Classical tradition invokes this for removal of vastu defects from home and workplace
Invokes the protection of directional deities (Dikpalas)
Traditionally associated with peace, prosperity, and positive energy to the house
Neutralizes negative energies caused by wrong construction
Protects inhabitants from misfortune and health issues
Ideal for recitation during Vastu Puja or housewarming
description: Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantra (वास्तु दोष निवारण मन्त्र) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This sacred composition is sourced from Rigveda - Vastu Suktam and Traditional and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to remove all Vastu defects from home and workplace, neutralize negative energies caused by wrong construction, and invoke the protection of directional deities (Dikpalas). This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Griha Pravesh for enhanced blessings. Comprising 9 verses, it can be completed in approximately 6 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantra in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Vedic mantras to remove Vastu defects and bring peace and prosperity to your home.

seoFaqs.question: What is Vastu Dosha and how do these mantras help?
seoFaqs.answer: Vastu Dosha refers to defects in the directional, dimensional or elemental alignment of a built structure that, according to Vastu Shastra, disturb the natural flow of cosmic and earthly energies and consequently affect the health, wealth, relationships and peace of mind of the residents. Common doshas include kitchens placed in the northeast, toilets in the brahmasthana (centre), entrances in inauspicious directions, and irregular plot shapes. The Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantras are Sanskrit invocations preserved in classical tradition — drawing from the Rigveda Vastu Sukta, the Matsya Purana and other Vastu-related texts — recited as a propitiatory remedy when structural correction is not possible. They invoke Vastu Purusha and the directional guardians (dik-palas) to bestow harmony despite the dosha. (Sources: Hindupedia Vastu Shastra; Matsya Purana; Drikpanchang Vastu Shanti.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the principal scriptural sources of these mantras?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vedic foundation is the Rigveda Vastoshpate Sukta (7.54-55) — the famous hymn addressed to Vastoshpati, the lord of the dwelling, asking him to bless the home with prosperity, accept the residents and protect them from harm. The procedural elaboration appears in classical Vastu Shastra texts including the Matsya Purana (chapters on Vastu Vidya), the Vishvakarma Vastu Shastra, the Mayamatam, the Manasara, and sections of the Brihat Samhita by Varahamihira. Puranic supplements appear in the Skanda Purana and Agni Purana, while Grihya Sutra paddhatis from Apastamba and Bodhayana provide the procedural framework for the Vastu Shanti homa. The mantras circulated today combine these sources in various paddhati compilations. (Sources: Hindupedia Vastu; Wikipedia Vastu Shastra; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantras be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal occasion is Griha Pravesh — the formal ceremony of moving into a new home — when the Vastu Shanti puja and homa are performed once at the auspicious muhurta to consecrate the space. Beyond this, the mantras are recited annually on the housewarming anniversary or during major festivals such as Akshaya Tritiya, Vijayadashami and Diwali. Daily recitation is recommended in homes that show signs of Vastu Dosha — recurrent illness, financial losses, family disputes, restless sleep, business failures despite hard work — particularly in the morning after the lamp-lighting and at the evening sandhya. The mantras may also be recited during renovation, when adding extensions, before installing new appliances in the kitchen, or when shifting offices. (Sources: Drikpanchang Vastu Shanti; Hindupedia; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Vastu Purusha and what role does he play in these mantras?
seoFaqs.answer: Vastu Purusha is the cosmic being who, according to puranic narrative, was created when the deities trapped a powerful asura beneath the earth and sat upon his body in a specific arrangement to keep him pinned. Brahma decreed that this being would henceforth be Vastu Purusha — lord of every plot of land — and that any structure built upon the earth must honour him by respecting the placement of the deities sitting on his body. The 81-pada Vastu Mandala diagrams the arrangement of these deities — Brahma at the centre, the eight dik-palas around the perimeter, Surya and other devas in specific positions. The Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantras invoke Vastu Purusha along with all 45 deities of the mandala to grant peace and bless the structure. (Sources: Matsya Purana; Hindupedia Vastu Purusha; Wikipedia Vastu Shastra.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantra promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources promise that consistent recitation of the Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantras propitiates Vastu Purusha and the directional guardians, bringing harmony to the household even when structural defects cannot be corrected. Specific benefits include peace and emotional stability among family members, prosperity and steady cash flow, freedom from recurrent illness and accidents, restful sleep, success in business conducted from the premises, protection from theft and natural disaster, and the gradual neutralisation of negative energy accumulated from the dosha. Combined with practical Vastu remedies — yantra installation, mirror placement, plant and water-feature corrections — the mantras are considered one of the most respected Vastu remedies in classical tradition. (Sources: Hindupedia; Drikpanchang; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: Can Vastu mantras be chanted by householders without a priest?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. While the formal Vastu Shanti puja and homa traditionally require a qualified purohit familiar with the homa-paddhati and the family's grihya-sutra lineage, the daily devotional recitation of the basic Vastu Dosh Nivaran Mantras — particularly the Vastoshpate Sukta and the namaskara to Vastu Purusha — can be performed by any householder after the morning bath, facing east or northeast, with hands folded before the household altar. Many practitioners also include these mantras during the daily lamp-lighting at sunrise and sunset. The full Vastu Shanti homa with agni, samidha, ghee oblations and the consecration of a Vastu yantra does require a trained pandit. (Sources: Hindupedia Vastu; Drikpanchang; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: Do Vastu mantras eliminate the need for structural Vastu correction?
seoFaqs.answer: Classical sources are clear that mantras propitiate Vastu Purusha but do not magically eliminate the underlying physical defect. Where major structural problems exist — kitchen in the northeast, toilet in the brahmasthana, main door facing an inauspicious direction — the orthodox advice is to undertake correction where feasible (relocation, adjustment, screening) and to use mantras as supplementary propitiation. Where correction is not feasible due to cost, rental status or municipal restrictions, the mantras and yantra installation become the principal remedy. The Vastu Shanti homa performed at Griha Pravesh is considered the most efficacious propitiation, and an annual repetition is recommended. Mantras work best as part of an integrated remedy alongside ethical conduct, cleanliness and devotional discipline. (Sources: Hindupedia Vastu; Sanatan Sanstha; Drikpanchang.)
